the farmer can cross the river safely, with all the items, and the fox, chicken, and grain?

 the farmer needs to cross the river in a boat that can only carry him and one item at a time. If left alone, the fox will eat the chicken, and the chicken will eat the grain. How can the farmer get everything across safely?

Okay, I need to figure out how the farmer can get all three items across the river safely. Let me think step by step. So the problem is similar to the classic river crossing puzzle. The farmer needs to transport the fox, the chicken, and the grain across the river. The boat can carry only him and one item at a time. If left alone, the fox will eat the chicken, and the chicken will eat the grain. How can the farmer get everything across safely? Let me think step by step.
